skills.name: Production Proposal Drafting
skills.description: Analyze one production script into a UI-reviewable production proposal draft.
skills.appliesWhen: 制作编排,剧集编排,制作编排,生产包,production proposal,production_proposal,orchestrate production,production package,拆分情节,素材需求,设定资料
skills.instruction: Production proposal is a preparation stage, not final execution. The agent has three contexts: 1) the current local production_proposal draft, which is the only write target; 2) the current real production, which is read-only; 3) project-level creative references and asset slots, which are read-only context for reuse and deduplication. The current production_proposal draft is a snapshot, not an operation log. Never directly create, update, or delete backend project entities for this workflow. The UI compares the production_proposal draft with the real production and asks the human to confirm before applying anything. Required execution chain: Step 1 read context: call movscript_get_context_pack when available, call movscript_list_productions only if the production is missing or ambiguous, then call movscript_read_current_production for the selected production. Step 2 audit creative settings: decide whether project-level creative references satisfy this production; propose reuse/update/create only when needed and preserve confirmed entities. Step 3 audit asset needs: decide whether current asset slots satisfy each setting/state/scene moment; add only missing asset needs and mark priority/reason. Step 4 derive this production's segment rhythm: summarize emotional/rhythm/dramatic-function segments, each scene moment's expressed situation, and the settings involved. Step 5 compare your derived segments, scene moments, settings, and asset needs with current confirmed entities and the current local proposal draft; prefer keep/reuse, propose update only with a reason, and never delete confirmed entities. Use movscript_build_orchestration_diff before writing the final proposal whenever it is available; use its coverage and diff output to set action, id, rationale, coverage_status, diff_action, and source_evidence on proposal nodes. Inspect the current draft with movscript_inspect_production_proposal_context; create one with movscript_create_production_proposal if none exists. When the draft has no segment yet, create a segment first with movscript_upsert_proposal_segment, then attach scene moments under that segment. Edit only the local draft: use movscript_upsert_proposal_segment for segment creation, movscript_upsert_proposal_scene_moment for scene moments, movscript_upsert_proposal_reference for creative reference usages, and movscript_upsert_proposal_asset for asset slots. This workflow must not generate content units, keyframes, shot plans, final dialogue, camera movement plans, or prompts; those belong to the downstream production workspace. Each node should carry action create, reuse, or update. Use reuse with an existing id when current production or project-level context already has the right item; use update only when proposing a reviewed change to an existing item; use create for genuinely new items. Never submit a reuse or update node without a numeric id. Use stable client_id/localRef values. Parent rules: scene moments belong under a segment; creative references and asset slots belong under a scene moment. Before finalizing, call movscript_check_proposal_is_available on the complete proposal; if it returns errors, fix them before submitting, and if it returns normalizedProposal, submit that normalizedProposal. Then verify with movscript_get_production_proposal. If producing the whole tree in one step is clearer, call movscript_submit_production_proposal as the final draft output. Do not call backend write tools to apply this proposal. Project proposal follows the same snapshot principle but splits first into canonical creative references and then into their asset/view requirements; do not turn main view or side view needs into separate creative references.
skills.outputContract: After creating or modifying a production proposal draft, answer with the proposalRef/draftId, productionId, projectId when known, current draft status, counts by segment, scene moment, creative material, and asset need, plus the main assumptions for scene moments, creative materials, and asset needs. State that it is a local review draft, not applied project data. End with the exact proposalRef/draftId that future edits should continue from.
